summary: Paper Trading feature fully tested (backend + frontend). All 21 backend pytest cases PASSED. All frontend flows verified: PAPER tab, portfolio stats, Auto Execute toggle, Positions/History/Order tabs, Trade button on scanner signals with pre-fill, close position modal, risk/reward calculator. One pre-existing chart error identified (unrelated to paper trading).

ui_bugs.issue: Console error: 'TypeError: chart.addPriceLine is not a function' fires repeatedly when stock is loaded. Pre-existing bug in lightweight-charts integration, not related to paper trading.

issues: Initial balance displays as '₹500.6K' or '₹472.0K' based on live state - shows ₹500.0K only after a fresh reset. This is correct behavior but user expectation may need ₹500.0K on very first visit (already handled via _ensure_paper_portfolio on backend).

passed_tests: Backend: POST /api/paper-trade/reset returns 200 with initial_balance=500000
Backend: GET /api/paper-trade/portfolio returns 200 with all required fields
Backend: GET /api/paper-trade/portfolio initial_balance = 500000
Backend: GET /api/paper-trade/portfolio current_balance = 500000 after reset
Backend: POST /api/paper-trade/order (BUY) returns 201 with trade_id, symbol, direction=BUY, status=OPEN
Backend: POST /api/paper-trade/order deducts invested_amount from balance correctly
Backend: POST /api/paper-trade/order (SELL) returns 201
Backend: POST /api/paper-trade/order with excessive quantity returns 400 (insufficient balance)
Backend: GET /api/paper-trade/positions returns 200 with 'positions' list
Backend: GET /api/paper-trade/positions shows placed orders with correct fields
Backend: GET /api/paper-trade/history returns 200 with 'trades' list
Backend: PUT /api/paper-trade/close/{trade_id} returns 200 with status/pnl/exit_price
Backend: PUT /api/paper-trade/close/{trade_id} updates portfolio balance correctly
Backend: PUT /api/paper-trade/close/{trade_id} moves trade to history
Backend: PUT /api/paper-trade/close/{nonexistent} returns 404
Backend: total_trades count increases after close
Backend: winning_trades count increases on profitable close
Backend: TARGET_HIT status when exit >= target for BUY
Backend: SL_HIT status when exit <= stop_loss for BUY with negative PnL
Frontend: PAPER tab visible in right sidebar (alongside SCANNER, STRATEGIES, GHOST, MONTE CARLO)
Frontend: Paper Trading panel loads with all 5 stat cards (available-balance, total-pnl, win-rate, open-count, total-trades)
Frontend: Available Balance shows ₹500.0K/₹500K initial display after reset
Frontend: Auto Execute toggle found and turns purple when clicked ON
Frontend: Positions tab shows empty state with helpful text ('Koi open position nahi')
Frontend: History tab shows closed trades with status badges (SL_HIT/TARGET_HIT/CLOSED)
Frontend: + New Order tab shows complete form (Symbol, Direction BUY/SELL, Quantity, Entry, SL red, Target green)
Frontend: Risk/Reward calculator appears when entry + SL + target filled (shows Risk, R:R ratio 1:2.0, Reward)
Frontend: Place Order submits successfully → auto-navigates to Positions tab → position shows in list
Frontend: Scanner signals show 3 Trade buttons (onPaperTrade prop working correctly in AutoScanner)
Frontend: Clicking Trade button on scanner signal → switches to PAPER tab → pre-fills Symbol=RELIANCE.NS, Entry=1366.5, SL=1325.5, Target=1421.16
Frontend: Close Position modal opens with Symbol, Direction, Qty, Exit Price pre-filled (live market price)
Frontend: Confirm Close button closes modal, shows 'Position closed!' toast, moves trade to History
action_items: LOW PRIORITY: Fix pre-existing chart.addPriceLine error in ChartPanel (TypeError: chart.addPriceLine is not a function) - occurs when stock is loaded, lightweight-charts API compatibility issue
critical_code_review_comments: get_paper_portfolio() and get_paper_positions() both call yf.Ticker().history() synchronously inside async endpoints for each open position - this blocks the event loop under load. Wrap in asyncio.to_thread() for production use.
Portfolio uses a single 'default' portfolio_id - no multi-user isolation. This is acceptable for MVP but note it's a single shared portfolio for all users.
server.py is now ~9332 lines - far exceeds 700-line guideline. Paper trading code (lines 5753-5958) should be extracted to services/paper_trading.py module.
_ensure_paper_portfolio() does a find_one + insert_one in two separate async DB calls without a transaction - could cause duplicate portfolio docs if two requests race. Add upsert pattern instead.

PaperTradingPanel.jsx: The pendingPaperTrade useEffect dependency array omits selectedStock but references it (line 239) - should add selectedStock to deps or use useCallback properly to avoid stale closure.
seed_data_creation: Test trades placed during backend pytest run (reset + place/close cycle). Portfolio reset at test start for clean state.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Paper Trading MVP fully verified in iteration_6. Backend test file: /app/backend/tests/test_paper_trading.py (21 tests, all pass). Key selectors: data-testid='paper-trading-panel', 'tab-paper', 'paper-tab-positions', 'paper-tab-history', 'paper-tab-order', 'auto-execute-toggle', 'order-symbol', 'order-entry', 'order-sl', 'order-target', 'place-order-btn', 'paper-trade-btn-{idx}' (Trade buttons on scanner signals). API: all 6 paper-trade endpoints working. Pre-existing bug: chart.addPriceLine TypeError in ChartPanel (not paper trading related).

rca_of_issues.chart_addPriceLine_error: ChartPanel uses chart.addPriceLine() (TradingView Lightweight Charts method) but the installed version may not support this method or it has been renamed/removed. Error fires for each price level overlay. RCA: lightweight-charts API version mismatch. Fix: use series.createPriceLine() or check API documentation for the installed version.
